# Documentation Requirements Quality Checklist: Package Features and Details Document

**Purpose**: Validate that the requirements in spec.md for the features document deliverable are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and coverage-ready (unit tests for requirements, not implementation).  
**Created**: 2025-03-14  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command. It tests whether the *requirements* are well-written and ready for implementation—not whether the implementation works.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for the exact location and format of the deliverable (one repo file + published page)? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities – Document]
- [x] CHK002 Is the set of theme groups (access modes, CLI, locale/fallback, migration, Catalog, platforms) explicitly enumerated in requirements or a referenced contract? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Clarifications]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements specified for the setup/guideline link target (create-if-missing and where it lives)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Edge Cases – Setup/guideline doc]
- [x] CHK004 Is the relationship between README and the features document (canonical source, link or summarize, no full duplication) fully specified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Edge Cases – Overlap with README]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements defined for how “in development” features must be presented (e.g. explicit label or note)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Edge Cases – Features in development]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “find a specific feature or topic quickly” quantified or bounded (e.g. time or structural criterion)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK007 Is “what it is / when to use it” defined so authors know the required content shape per feature? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK008 Is “minimal how to enable” defined (e.g. one line or one link) so it is not ambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK009 Is “reflect the package’s scope” tied to an explicit source (constitution, public docs) so verifiable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK010 Are “evaluators” and “adopters” defined or scoped so acceptance criteria can be applied consistently? [Clarity, Spec §User Stories, Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Is the deliverable path consistent across spec, plan, and tasks (e.g. doc/reference/features.md)? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities – Document]
- [x] CHK012 Do FR-002 (per-feature content + link to setup/guideline) and the edge case “setup/guideline doc” align without conflict? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK013 Are success criteria (SC-001–SC-004) aligned with the functional requirements so that satisfying FRs implies meeting SCs? [Consistency, Spec §Success Criteria, §Functional Requirements]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 Can “reader can determine whether the package supports their use case within 5 minutes” be verified without implementation (e.g. by review or dry-run)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK015 Can “every listed feature has a clear description of what it does and when it applies” be checked against a defined checklist? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK016 Is “covers all capabilities described in the project constitution and existing public documentation” traceable to an enumerated source list? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK017 Can “structure supports incremental updates” be verified (e.g. by describing a scenario where one section is added or changed)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are requirements defined for the evaluator scenario (find feature list and decide fit without reading code)? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for the adopter scenario (use document alone to enable one capability)? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements defined for the maintainer scenario (add or update one feature without rewriting the doc)? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK021 Is the “both audiences equally” (evaluators and adopters) requirement reflected in measurable acceptance criteria? [Coverage, Spec §Clarifications, Assumptions]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are requirements specified for when a feature is in development (what must appear so readers are not misled)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-006, Edge Cases – Features in development]
- [x] CHK023 Is the behavior when the setup/guideline page does not exist (create it) explicitly required and traceable to tasks? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-002, Edge Cases – Setup/guideline doc]
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements for README/doc home updates (link or summarize, no full duplication) stated so implementers know what to change? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases – Overlap with README]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK025 Is the assumption “repo file is source of truth for the published page” stated and consistent with FR-005? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-005]
- [x] CHK026 Is the dependency on the project constitution (for scope and feature list) explicit so validators know where to check alignment? [Dependency, Spec §FR-004, §SC-003]

## Ambiguities & Gaps

- [x] CHK027 Is “current package features” defined or tied to a source (constitution, README) so “all” can be verified? [Gap, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK028 Are requirements for nav/README/doc home updates (discoverability) explicit in the spec or only in tasks? [Traceability, Spec §User Story 3, tasks.md]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Reference spec sections (e.g. §FR-001) when verifying; use [Gap] if the requirement is missing or underspecified
- This checklist validates *requirement quality*; it does not verify that the built document or implementation is correct
